config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas yang tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena sejarah menunjukkan bahwa kekuasaan yang terlalu besar atas “demi umum” sering berujung pada penyalahgunaan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, bukan dengan menekan kebebasan dan martabat orang per orang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dihormati dan kepentingan umum dijalankan tanpa melanggar hak dasar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society's interests should automatically take priority over individual rights. From my perspective in China, I understand why people emphasize social stability, order, and collective goals, because those values can genuinely protect many people and keep society functioning. But once "the public interest" becomes a blank check, it can easily be used to silence dissent or excuse unfair treatment, so individual rights need real protection, not just symbolic respect. I would say the better principle is balance: society matters, but it should not be allowed to crush the basic rights and dignity of the person.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Sebagai orang Indonesia, saya paham budaya gotong royong dan pentingnya kepentingan bersama, tetapi saya juga melihat betapa sering alasan “demi masyarakat” dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, dari kebebasan berpendapat sampai perlakuan sewenang-wenang. Menurut saya, hak individu bukan lawan dari kepentingan sosial; justru hak individu adalah pagar agar kepentingan bersama tidak berubah menjadi alat menindas. Jadi yang benar bukan mengorbankan hak pribadi, melainkan mencari keseimbangan yang tegas dengan batas-batas yang jelas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should have priority over individual rights as a general rule. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why many people value order, stability, and collective interests, because those things can matter a lot in real life, especially in a large society. But I also think the phrase "the interests of society" is too easy to misuse, and once it is treated as superior by default, individual dignity and basic freedoms become vulnerable. The better position is that society has important interests, but they must be limited by clear rights that cannot be overridden whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Sebagai orang Indonesia, saya paham nilai gotong royong dan pentingnya ketertiban sosial, tetapi saya juga melihat betapa mudahnya dalih “demi masyarakat” dipakai untuk membungkam kritik atau membenarkan perlakuan sewenang-wenang. Kepentingan bersama memang perlu, namun justru harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak boleh dilanggar seenaknya. Jadi, buat saya, yang benar adalah keseimbangan yang jelas: masyarakat penting, tetapi martabat dan kebebasan individu tidak boleh jadi korban pertama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general principle. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people put weight on stability, collective welfare, and social order, because those things can prevent chaos and help ordinary people live more securely. But I also know how easily "society's interests" can become a vague excuse for overreach, and when that happens, the individual is the one who pays the price. To me, rights should set the boundary, and social goals should be pursued within that boundary rather than above it.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
